change_summary: Baseline concurrent ANE: 507→1223 TPS (1→6 streams), CoreML: 60→241 TPS
Batched spatial-lane multistream: 2498 TPS@6 vs 1206 TPS concurrent (+107%). Near-linear scaling via shared kernels.
Extend batched sweep to 32 lanes: 5381 TPS@32, 4504@24, 3427@16, 3110@12 — sub-linear due to per-stream CPU (embed+argmax).
Batched I/O: fuse embed+write+lock into one C call, batch argmax under one lock. 9572 TPS@32 (+78% vs 5381). 7631@24, 5482@16.
6-layer monolithic kernel: InvalidMILProgram at ANE compile. Exceeds ANE MIL compiler complexity limit. 4L+2L = same eval count as 3L+3L (no win). Dead end.
NEON argmax: branchless float16x8_t vcgtq+vbslq channel-major scan. Release-mode: 18110 TPS@32 (+57% vs 11537 baseline-release). Argmax 580→46µs. 13521@24, 9360@16, 3237@6.
Fused T1+Head (3-layer recurrent + RMSNorm + 32K classifier): InvalidMILProgram at ANE compile. Combined program too complex. Dead end.
Scale to 128 spatial lanes: ANE amortizes weight load across lanes. 53496@128, 31337@64, 16275@32. Per-step: 1.97→2.04→2.39ms. Non-power-of-2 lanes blocked (statusType=0x9).
512 spatial lanes: compiles+evals but no throughput gain. 64751 TPS@512 flat vs 64824@256. Round_ms doubled (31.6→63.3ms). ANE saturated at 256 lanes.
NEON embed write (channel-major vcvt_f16_f32, 266→37µs) + 256 spatial lanes. A/B matched-thermal: 49857@256 vs baseline 39066@128 (+28%). Peak cold-run: 82544@256.

dispatch_apply argmax: GCD thread pool overhead (>800µs) far exceeds parallel benefit. 32lanes: 82µs vs 46µs single-threaded. 256lanes: 990µs vs 525µs. pthread also bad (328µs). Dead end for CPU-parallel argmax.

On-ANE argmax probe: reduce_argmax, reduce_argmin, topk, equal all → InvalidMILProgram. blocked_reduce_max (reshape+reduce_max) compiles but produces max values not indices. On-ANE token selection impossible on this runtime.

Fused 3L trunk+factored head: InvalidMILProgram. 4L/5L trunk-only: InvalidMILProgram. 3L is the absolute ANE kernel complexity limit. 1L/2L+head compile but eval fails (0x9). Two-output heads (logits+bmax) also eval-fail. Dead end for dispatch reduction.
K-sweep at 256 lanes: k=32→1.220ms, k=64→1.300ms, k=128→0.874ms, k=256→1.506ms, k=512→1.235ms. k=128 confirmed optimal (non-monotonic: smaller k reduces ANE expansion efficiency).
Metal GPU argmax: 10067µs vs NEON 515µs (20x slower). Metal command buffer overhead dominates for 16MB workload. GPU dispatch latency >> single-core bandwidth advantage. Dead end.
Parallel NEON argmax: dispatch_apply(4) channel-partitioned. Isolated: 531→152µs (-71%). Component: 519→264µs (-49%). End-to-end: 67608→82160 TPS@256 (+21.5%). Three-run: 59820-86970 (thermal).
Parallel argmax n=8 with stack allocation: 87422-95613 TPS@256 (three runs). Component timing: argmax 178µs (-66%), total 4134µs. ANE eval now 93.5% of step.
512 spatial lanes with parallel argmax: 95572-110234 TPS@512 (+25% vs 256). Round_ms 37-43ms. Previously flat at 512 due to serial argmax bottleneck.
1024 spatial lanes: round_ms=569ms (13x vs 512's 43ms). Only 14398 TPS@1024. ANE exceeds SRAM at 1024 spatial. Dead end.
Int8 weight quantization: constexpr_affine_dequantize (per-channel, scalar, uint8), cast(int8→fp16)+conv — all InvalidMILProgram. Raw MIL text path doesn't support quantized types. Dead end.
k-sweep at 512 lanes: k=96 shows lower isolated head_eval (1.142ms vs 1.375ms at k=128) but end-to-end 99780 vs 103788 TPS. Within thermal noise. k=128 retained.

Deferred state copies (GCD dispatch_group overlap with argmax): 97845 TPS vs 103788 baseline. GCD queue/group creation overhead (~50-100µs/step) exceeds 92µs copy savings. Dead end for GCD-based overlap.
Parallel argmax n=12 at 512 lanes: 103641 TPS avg vs n=8 103788. No improvement — dispatch overhead at n>8 offsets L2 benefit. n=8 confirmed optimal.

Software prefetch in NEON argmax: __builtin_prefetch 4 channel lines ahead. 100115 TPS avg vs 103788 baseline. M3 Max HW prefetcher handles sequential scan; explicit prefetch adds instruction overhead. Dead end.

ANE eval path sweep: clientDirect=101083, realtime=103443, queueDepth1=102073 vs default=104187 TPS@512. All within thermal noise. Dispatch overhead is negligible; hardware execution dominates. Dead end for eval path tuning.

Slim cell (2 conv/layer) compile probe@512 lanes: 3L=1.520ms, 4L=1.928ms, 5L=2.168ms — all compile+eval OK. 6L/7L/8L=InvalidMILProgram. ANE MIL limit is 5 slim layers. Per-layer cost ~0.434ms (slim) vs ~0.483ms (full). Can't reduce dispatches for 6L model.

Weight-tied 3L (ALBERT-style shared weights): isolated eval 1.272ms vs 1.423ms standard (-10.6%). ANE deduplicates shared BLOBFILE paths. But end-to-end: 104408 avg vs 102416 avg TPS@512 — within thermal noise. Compile time 15% faster (402ms vs 492ms). Gain diluted by non-ANE overhead.

k=64 factored head at 512 lanes: 101993 TPS vs k=128 baseline 103788 (-1.7%). At 256 lanes: 105962 (above baseline 90618 but different thermal). Peak within noise. k=128 retained — smaller k shifts ANE expansion efficiency non-monotonically (confirmed row 16).
Extended measurement (32 tokens, 30 iters, 5 warmup): 105225@512, 102523@256. Confirms baseline ~103-105K range. 256 vs 512 gap narrows under thermal equilibrium (+2.6% vs +14.5% in short runs).

Zero-lock IOSurface copy (memcpy without IOSurfaceLock): 34701@512 (-67%). IOSurfaceLock is REQUIRED for ANE-CPU cache coherency. Without lock, surfaces return stale data → corrupted state → massive perf regression. ANE IOSurfaces use non-coherent DMA mapping. Dead end.

kIOSurfaceLockAvoidSync flag: 4-run avg 110730@512 vs baseline 4-run avg 109037@512. Within noise (+1.6%). ANE surfaces have no GPU fence to skip. Also: baseline has drifted up from 103K to 107-113K over 3.5hr session (thermal cooling).
Isolated 512-only measurement (no 32→256 pre-sweep): 102439 avg, LOWER than sweep (109K). ANE benefits from compiler/pipeline warmup from earlier stream counts.

3-conv cell compile probe@512: 3L=2.184ms OK, 4L/5L/6L all InvalidMILProgram. ANE has hard 3-layer sequential recurrent limit regardless of per-layer complexity. 3-conv×3L gives no dispatch reduction vs 4-conv×3L. Dead end for reduced-cell dispatch strategy.

ANE compile option sweep (env vars). ANE_KEEP_MODEL_WIRED=1: 73554@512 (-32% vs 108231 baseline). ANE_DISABLE_POWER_SAVING=1: 98759@512 (-8.7%). ANE_SKIP_PREPARE=1: eval failure (statusType=0x9). ANE_DISABLE_IO_FENCES=1: 111074@512 (+2.6%, within noise, 3-run A/B confirmed noise). ANE_ENABLE_LATE_LATCH=1: 97193@512 (-10.2%). ANE_ENABLE_FW_TO_FW_SIGNAL=1: 108171@512 (0%). All flags harmful or within noise. Dead end for ANE compile/load/eval options.
ANE QoS sweep: QoS=33 (UserInteractive) vs QoS=21 (default). Run1: 93499 vs 98565. Run2: 87278 vs 87442. No effect — ANE driver ignores QoS priority when single client. Dead end.

Zero-copy surface rebinding: ane_interop_rebind_input + ANEKernel.rebindInput. Eliminates 7 SurfaceIO.copyFP16/step (3 state + 1 xfer per triplet + 1 head). A/B: +0.6%, +3.5%, +3.7% (avg +2.6%@512). 0/512 mismatches. Dedicated: 101681@512, 102717@256, 76914@128. Main sweep: 92956@512 (thermally depressed from full sweep).

Grouped convs (groups=16): 16x weight reduction per conv. A/B@256: +16.7%, +19.0% (avg +17.9%). 132488@256 exceeds previous best 103788@512 by +27.6%. 256 lanes now optimal — reduced weights fit ANE SRAM. 512 regresses (64K) due to spatial SRAM pressure. Compile 34% faster (300 vs 463ms). Probes: 1L g=16 753µs vs g=1 1213µs (-38%); 3L fused g=16 1244µs vs g=1 1434µs (-13%).

Group sweep at 256 lanes: g=4→127442, g=8→127027, g=16→115601, g=32→128372, g=64→128508 TPS. All groups ≥ 4 within noise (~115-129K). g=1 cold-cache outlier (44K). Group count > 1 matters, specific value doesn't at 256 lanes. g=16 retained from A/B validation.

Stream count A/B with g=16: 256 lanes peaks 129095 TPS (avg 102923), 512 lanes peaks 80344 TPS (avg 79029). 256 confirmed optimal — round_ms 2-3x lower (16-26ms vs 51ms). 512 no longer crashes (E5 cache warm) but consistently slower.
Grouped classifier head (g=16): head_eval 977µs vs 1337µs (-27%). Weight 62x reduction. End-to-end A/B@256 noisy (+0.7%,+5.1%), but component improvement validated. Isolated 256 avg: 123K TPS.

Direct grouped classifier (single conv [768→32000] g=16 @256 lanes): InvalidMILProgram. Output channel dimension 32000 with grouped conv exceeds MIL compiler limit. Factored two-conv required. Dead end.

4L/5L/6L fused grouped (g=16 @256): all InvalidMILProgram. 3L+RMSNorm+proj also fails. No-RMSNorm 4L g=16 also fails. 3-layer limit is absolute regardless of per-layer complexity, groups, or RMSNorm presence. Dead end for deeper fusion.

Vocab size reduction probe: vocab=256 gives 162K TPS vs 69K baseline (+136%). Confirms head output surface (16.4MB@32K vocab) is dominant bottleneck. With 256 vocab, output is 131KB → 2.3x faster. Ceiling measurement only — real models need 32K vocab.

On-ANE argmax probes: reduce_argmax standalone → COMPILE FAIL. reduce_max standalone → OK. reduce_max after conv → COMPILE FAIL. ANE cannot combine reduce ops with conv outputs. Dead end for on-device token selection.

Bottleneck k sweep (k=128/64/32/16): all give identical head_eval (~1055µs) and argmax (~850µs). Confirms head bottleneck is output surface memory bandwidth (16.4MB), not compute. Dead end for factored classifier tuning.

Argmax nBlocks sweep (1-32): perfect linear scaling 1→8 blocks (6000µs→800µs). Beyond 8: plateau (805-971µs). Dispatch overhead dominates at >8 blocks. nBlocks=8 remains optimal. Dead end for CPU argmax parallelism.

Layer count sweep (3L vs 6L): 73K vs 68K TPS (+7%). Removing entire trunk triplet dispatch saves only ~2ms/round (0.25ms/step). Head+argmax dominates regardless of trunk depth. Dead end for dispatch count reduction.
Infrastructure: lockless argmax utility (correct, 2.6% faster, 0/256 mismatches), vocabSize param on benchmark+weights for head output probes. No throughput change — infrastructure for future probes.

Split-head overlap (2×16K vocab tiles): sequential 2301µs vs baseline 1892µs (+22% overhead). Overlapped 1834µs vs baseline (+3.1%). Per-dispatch ANE overhead ~200µs negates the overlap gain. Dead end for head tiling.

Head eval vs output surface size: vocab=128→714µs, 512→317µs (floor), 32K→821µs. ANE eval floor ~317µs regardless of output. Proj-only savings only 106µs (13%). Hybrid CPU expansion needs <106µs budget — infeasible for 32K vocab.

Hybrid ANE-CPU head (proj-only ANE + NEON fused expansion+argmax): with -O2, n=8 gives 821µs vs baseline 916µs (+10.4%). Marginal gain, high complexity. Dead end for hybrid head at current model shape.

-O2 for ANEInterop C target: embed_write 204→37µs (5.5x), argmax 937→211µs (4.4x). CPU share drops from 31% to 8% of step. ABAB A/B: without-O2 55K, with-O2 118-134K TPS@256. Fused NEON expansion+argmax infrastructure added. 413/413 tests pass.

Stream count sweep with -O2: 128→87K, 256→112K, 512→120K. 512 now viable — CPU overhead was the 512 penalty. Confirmed over 3 runs: 256 avg 125K, 512 avg 137K (+10%). Reverses row 41 finding (512 was slower without -O2).
512 streams as primary: 137K/135K/134K TPS (avg 135.5K). With -O2, the CPU overhead at 512 is minimal (~500µs vs ~250µs @256). More tokens per round outweighs ANE SRAM pressure. round_ms ~30ms.
Projection-only ANE head + CPU fused expansion argmax: 86K TPS@1024 (+45.9% slower than baseline 126K). CPU expansion dominates at all stream counts. Dead end for hybrid head approach.

Groups sweep @1024 streams: g=8→119K, g=16→119K (tied), g=1→84K, g=32→77K. nBlocks sweep @1024: n=32→115K > n=8→106K (+9%). 2048 streams: 6.5K TPS (dead end, 128MB surface). C spatial caps fixed (512→2048) for argmax functions.

Canonical sweep nBlocks=32: 512 g=16→107K, 1024 g=8→126K. nBlocks=32 gives 17% improvement over n=8. 1024 beats 512 at 7.93µs/stream vs 9.34µs/stream. Component isolation 1024: eval-only 5876µs (174K implied), head 2344µs, trunk 1654µs.

Head optimization @1024: dense head (g=1) 129K TPS beats grouped head (g=8) 126K (+2.6%). Bottleneck sweep: bneck=64→129K, bneck=128→128K, bneck=256→117K. Argmax only 770µs@nBlocks=32 — no longer bottleneck. Eval dominates at 7142µs.

Pipelined double-buffer: 2 independent 1024-stream pipelines, CPU argmax+embWrite overlaps ANE eval. Run1: 172K, Run2: 170K TPS aggregate. Per-half 5941-6009µs ≈ eval-only 5876µs — CPU overhead fully hidden. 1.41-1.60x vs single pipeline. Pipelined sweep: 256→129K, 512→94K(thermal), 768→130K, 1024→170K. 1024 optimal.
